skill.name: churn-prevention
skill.description: When the user wants to reduce churn, build cancellation flows, set up save offers, recover failed payments, or implement retention strategies. Also use when the user mentions 'churn,' 'cancel flow,' 'offboarding,' 'save offer,' 'dunning,' 'failed payment recovery,' 'win-back,' 'retention,' 'exit survey,' 'pause subscription,' 'involuntary churn,' 'people keep canceling,' 'churn rate is too high,' 'how do I keep users,' or 'customers are leaving.' Use this whenever someone is losing subscribers or wants to build systems to prevent it. For post-cancel win-back email sequences, see email-sequence. For in-app upgrade paywalls, see paywall-upgrade-cro.
skill.summary: Build cancel flows, save offers, dunning sequences, and retention strategies to reduce SaaS churn.
skill.author: Corey Haines

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists of markdown documentation (SKILL.md, references/*.md) and JSON test cases (evals/evals.json) with no executable code. Pattern matches for 'weak cryptographic algorithm', 'shell backtick execution', 'path traversal', and 'reconnaissance' occur in documentation examples and test assertion text, not in runnable code. The skill provides legitimate SaaS retention guidance including cancel flow design, dunning email sequences, and payment recovery strategies.

content.user_title: Reduce SaaS Churn with Cancel Flows and Retention Strategies
content.value_statement: Losing subscribers hurts growth and valuation. This skill helps you build effective cancel flows, save offers, and dunning sequences to reduce both voluntary and involuntary churn.
actual_capabilities: Design multi-step cancel flows with exit surveys and dynamic save offers
Build dunning email sequences for failed payment recovery
Create customer health scores from usage and engagement signals
Map save offers to specific cancellation reasons
Set up proactive retention interventions before customers cancel
Configure smart retry logic for payment failures
limitations: Requires integration with billing provider (Stripe, Chargebee, etc.) for implementation
Cancel flow UI requires frontend development or third-party retention platform
Dunning emails need email delivery infrastructure (Customer.io, Mailchimp, etc.)
Health scoring requires product usage data collection and analytics setup

use_cases.title: B2C SaaS reducing price-sensitive churn
use_cases.description: A $29/month productivity app with 7% monthly churn builds a cancel flow offering discounts and pauses, achieving 28% save rate.

use_cases.title: B2B platform preventing high-value account loss
use_cases.description: A project management tool implements MRR-based routing to send $500+/month accounts to customer success before cancellation completes.

use_cases.title: Subscription service recovering failed payments
use_cases.description: An e-commerce subscription box sets up dunning emails and smart retries, recovering 55% of failed payments automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Design a basic cancel flow
prompt_templates.prompt: We have a SaaS product at $49/month with about 2000 customers and 6% monthly churn. Design a cancel flow from scratch including the exit survey questions, save offers, and confirmation experience.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize existing save offers
prompt_templates.prompt: Our cancel flow saves 15% of customers but industry benchmark is 25-30%. We currently offer a 20% discount for 2 months. Analyze our offer strategy and recommend improvements based on cancellation reasons.

prompt_templates.title: Build dunning sequence for failed payments
prompt_templates.prompt: We lose about $8000 MRR monthly to failed credit card payments. Create a complete dunning strategy including pre-dunning prevention, smart retry schedule, 4-email dunning sequence, and grace period policy.

prompt_templates.title: Create health score model with interventions
prompt_templates.product: B2B SaaS with team collaboration features
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 0-100 health score model using login frequency, feature adoption, support tickets, and NPS responses. Define risk tiers and specific interventions for each tier including email templates and in-app messages.

output_examples.input: 40% of cancellations cite 'too expensive' - what save offers should we test?
output: Temporary discount: 25% off for 3 months (saves 20-25% of price-sensitive cancels)
Downgrade option: Switch to basic plan at lower price point (saves 15-20%)
Annual billing switch: Prepay annually at 2 months free equivalent (improves cash flow, reduces churn)
Pause subscription: 1-2 month pause for temporary budget constraints (60-80% reactivate)
Follow-up: Add free-text field to uncover if 'too expensive' masks value concerns

output_examples.input: How should we structure dunning emails for a B2B audience?
output: Email 1 (Day 0): Friendly, matter-of-fact notification with direct payment link
Email 2 (Day 3): Helpful reminder emphasizing team impact and data preservation
Email 3 (Day 7): Clear urgency with specific consequences and deadline
Email 4 (Day 10): Final warning with reactivation path clearly stated
Tone: Professional, non-accusatory, focused on continuity not payment

best_practices: Match save offers to cancellation reasons - discounts don't save users who aren't using the product
Make cancellation easy to find - dark patterns damage brand trust and violate regulations
Address involuntary churn separately - failed payments need dunning, not save offers
anti_patterns: Showing the same discount to every cancellation reason
Requiring phone calls or excessive steps to cancel
Ignoring failed payment recovery while focusing only on voluntary churn
Offering discounts deeper than 30% (trains cancel-for-deal behavior)

faq.question: What is a good cancel flow save rate?
faq.answer: Industry average is 20-30%. Well-optimized flows with dynamic offers reach 30-40%. B2B with personal outreach can achieve 40-50%.

faq.question: Should we require a phone call to cancel?
faq.answer: No. FTC Click-to-Cancel rules and similar regulations in other regions require cancellation to be as easy as signup. Phone-only cancellation is illegal in many jurisdictions.

faq.question: How long should we allow paused subscriptions?
faq.answer: 1-3 months maximum. Pauses beyond 3 months rarely reactivate. Set automatic reactivation with advance notice and allow one pause per 12-month period.

faq.question: What recovery rate should we expect from dunning?
faq.answer: Target 50-60% overall recovery. Soft declines (insufficient funds) should recover at 70%+. Hard declines (stolen cards) recover at 20-40%.

faq.question: Should we degrade access during the grace period?
faq.answer: For B2B, maintain full access to preserve trust. For B2C, read-only access creates urgency without data loss fear. Never immediately lock out - this reduces recovery rates.

faq.question: How do we prevent customers from gaming pause or discount offers?
faq.answer: Limit pauses to once per 12 months. Track discount accepters and don't re-offer if they cancel again within 6 months. Cap discounts at 25-30% maximum.
